items.title: Generate a Quotation document
items.description: Emit a non-posting Quotation entity (header + line items). Header: customer, quotation date, valid-until date, currency, status (Draft/Submitted/Expired/Lost/Ordered). Lines: item, qty, rate, amount, tax template. No ledger or stock impact; it is a commercial offer only.
items.applicability: Sales module scaffolding when the generator is asked for a lead-to-cash flow.
items.example: Quotation QTN-2026-00001 for Customer ACME: 10 x WidgetA @ 100 = 1000, +15% tax = 1150, valid 30 days.
items.limitation: A quotation creates no financial or inventory obligation; converting it to a Sales Order is a separate explicit action and must copy, not link-mutate, the lines.

items.title: Generate a Sales Order document
items.description: Emit a Sales Order entity that confirms customer commitment. Header: customer, order date, delivery date, currency, status; optional link to source quotation. Lines carry ordered qty and a delivered/billed qty tracker. No GL posting; it drives downstream delivery and invoice fulfilment.
items.applicability: Order-management scaffolding; the anchor document for fulfilment tracking.
items.example: SO-2026-00001 confirms 10 x WidgetA; delivered_qty starts 0, billed_qty starts 0; status Submitted.
items.limitation: A Sales Order is not a financial document; over-delivery or over-billing guards must be implemented separately against the ordered qty.

items.title: Generate a Delivery Note with stock posting
items.description: Emit a Delivery Note that decrements inventory. On submit, post a stock-ledger issue (credit stock qty at valuation rate) and a GL entry Debit Cost of Goods Sold / Credit Inventory. Update the source Sales Order delivered_qty. Header carries customer, posting date, warehouse; lines carry item, qty, valuation rate.
items.applicability: Outbound logistics module; the point where goods leave the warehouse.
items.example: DN-2026-00001 ships 10 x WidgetA from WH-Main: stock -10; GL Dr COGS 600 / Cr Inventory 600.
items.limitation: Valuation rate must come from the stock valuation engine (e.g. moving average) at posting time; using the sales price as cost overstates COGS.

items.title: Generate a Sales Invoice with AR posting
items.description: Emit a Sales Invoice that posts revenue and receivable. On submit: Debit Accounts Receivable (gross), Credit Sales Revenue (net), Credit Output Tax (tax). Header: customer, posting date, due date, currency; lines: item, qty, rate, tax. Track outstanding_amount for payment allocation. Optionally update Sales Order billed_qty.
items.applicability: Billing module; the document that recognises revenue and creates the receivable.
items.example: SINV-2026-00001 for 1150: Dr AR 1150 / Cr Sales 1000 / Cr Output Tax 150; outstanding 1150, due in 30 days.
items.limitation: Revenue-recognition timing (delivery vs invoice) is policy-dependent; this rule assumes invoice-date recognition and does not implement deferred/accrued revenue schedules.

items.title: Generate a Payment Entry (receipt) with allocation
items.description: Emit a Payment Entry that records cash received and clears receivables. On submit: Debit Bank/Cash, Credit Accounts Receivable. Allocate the paid amount against one or more outstanding invoices, reducing each invoice's outstanding_amount. Header: party, payment date, mode, amount; allocation table maps amount to reference invoices.
items.applicability: Cash-application module for inbound customer payments.
items.example: PE-2026-00001 receives 1150: Dr Bank 1150 / Cr AR 1150; allocate 1150 to SINV-2026-00001, outstanding -> 0.
items.limitation: Partial payments, write-offs, and unallocated advances need explicit handling; an unallocated receipt must sit as a credit on the party account, not silently close an invoice.

items.title: Generate a Purchase Order document
items.description: Emit a Purchase Order that commits to buying from a supplier. Header: supplier, order date, expected receipt date, currency, status; lines: item, qty, rate, tax. No GL or stock impact; it authorises procurement and seeds received/billed qty trackers for three-way matching.
items.applicability: Procurement module; the anchor for the procure-to-pay flow.
items.example: PO-2026-00001 orders 100 x RawX @ 50 = 5000 from Supplier BETA; received_qty 0, billed_qty 0.
items.limitation: A PO does not reserve cash or stock; budget control and commitment accounting, if required, must be layered on separately.

items.title: Generate a Purchase Receipt with stock posting
items.description: Emit a Purchase Receipt that increases inventory on goods receipt. On submit: post a stock-ledger receipt (debit stock qty at purchase rate) and GL Debit Inventory / Credit Goods-Received-Not-Invoiced (GRNI). Update PO received_qty. Header: supplier, posting date, warehouse; lines: item, qty, rate.
items.applicability: Inbound logistics; recognises goods physically received before the bill arrives.
items.example: PR-2026-00001 receives 100 x RawX @ 50: stock +100; GL Dr Inventory 5000 / Cr GRNI 5000.
items.limitation: Using a GRNI/accrual account is required so inventory is recognised at receipt; clearing GRNI happens at invoice time and must reconcile to zero.

items.title: Generate a Purchase Invoice with AP posting
items.description: Emit a Purchase Invoice (supplier bill) that posts the payable. On submit: Debit GRNI or Expense/Inventory (net), Debit Input Tax, Credit Accounts Payable (gross). Track outstanding_amount. Where a Purchase Receipt exists, debit GRNI to clear the receipt accrual rather than re-debiting inventory.
items.applicability: Accounts-payable module; recognises the supplier liability.
items.example: PINV-2026-00001 for 5750: Dr GRNI 5000 / Dr Input Tax 750 / Cr AP 5750; outstanding 5750.
items.limitation: Three-way match (PO vs receipt vs invoice) on qty and rate is a separate control; without it the invoice can post amounts that diverge from what was ordered or received.

items.title: Generate a Supplier Payment Entry with allocation
items.description: Emit a Payment Entry for outbound supplier payment. On submit: Debit Accounts Payable, Credit Bank/Cash. Allocate against outstanding purchase invoices, reducing each invoice's outstanding_amount. Header: supplier, payment date, mode, amount; allocation table references the bills paid.
items.applicability: Disbursement module for paying supplier bills.
items.example: PE-2026-00002 pays 5750: Dr AP 5750 / Cr Bank 5750; allocate to PINV-2026-00001, outstanding -> 0.
items.limitation: Payment runs, hold/block flags, and bank reconciliation are out of scope here; this rule covers only the single allocated disbursement posting.

items.title: Generate a manual Journal Entry
items.description: Emit a general Journal Entry for adjustments not covered by a source document. Header: posting date, voucher type, remarks; lines: account, debit, credit, optional party/cost-centre. Enforce sum(debits)==sum(credits) before posting. This is the universal posting primitive all other documents ultimately compile down to.
items.applicability: Accounting core; depreciation, accruals, reclassifications, opening balances.
items.example: JE-2026-00001 accrues rent: Dr Rent Expense 1000 / Cr Accrued Liabilities 1000.
items.limitation: Free-form journals bypass sub-ledger controls, so they should be restricted by role and approval; unbalanced or wrong-account journals are a common audit finding.

items.title: Generate the Month-End Close routine
items.description: Emit a month-end close process: (1) post all accruals/prepayments, (2) reconcile bank and control accounts, (3) post depreciation, (4) value/reconcile inventory, (5) verify trial balance is in balance, (6) lock the period so no further posting is allowed to that month. Each step is a checklist gate, not a posting on its own.
items.applicability: Period-control module; recurring monthly governance task.
items.example: Close 2026-05: all sub-ledgers reconciled, trial balance balances to 0, period 2026-05 set to Locked.
items.limitation: Locking must be reversible by an authorised reopen with audit; a hard irreversible lock blocks legitimate late adjustments and audit corrections.

items.title: Generate the Fiscal-Year Close routine
items.description: Emit a year-end close that zeroes P&L accounts into retained earnings. Compute net profit/loss = total income - total expense for the fiscal year, post a closing Journal Entry transferring each P&L account balance to Retained Earnings, and carry Balance Sheet account balances forward as opening balances of the next year.
items.applicability: Annual close routine; the boundary between fiscal years.
items.example: FY2026 net profit 50000: Dr each income / Cr each expense / Cr Retained Earnings 50000; BS balances carried to FY2027 opening.
items.limitation: Only nominal (P&L) accounts are closed; real (Balance Sheet) accounts must carry forward. Closing real accounts to retained earnings is a serious error that destroys the balance sheet.

items.title: Generate the Trial Balance report
items.description: Emit a Trial Balance: for each ledger account, sum debits and credits over the selected period and show closing debit or credit balance. The grand total of all debit balances must equal the grand total of all credit balances. It is the primary proof that double-entry posting is internally consistent.
items.applicability: Financial-reporting module; the first report to validate after any posting work.
items.example: Trial Balance as at 2026-05-31: total debit balances 120000 = total credit balances 120000.
items.limitation: A balanced trial balance proves arithmetic consistency, not correctness: misclassified or compensating-error entries still balance. It is necessary, not sufficient.

items.title: Generate the Profit & Loss report
items.description: Emit a Profit & Loss statement covering income and expense (nominal) accounts for a period. Group by Income, Cost of Goods Sold, and Expense; compute Gross Profit (Income - COGS) and Net Profit (Gross Profit - Expenses). Source figures from GL period movements, never from opening balances.
items.applicability: Financial-reporting module; performance over a period.
items.example: P&L for FY2026: Income 200000 - COGS 120000 = Gross Profit 80000 - Expenses 30000 = Net Profit 50000.
items.limitation: P&L reflects accrual-basis period movement only; it must exclude Balance Sheet accounts. Cash-basis reporting requires a different aggregation and is not implied here.

items.title: Generate the Balance Sheet report
items.description: Emit a Balance Sheet of Asset, Liability and Equity accounts as at a date. Assets must equal Liabilities + Equity, where Equity includes retained earnings plus current-period net profit. Source closing balances from cumulative GL movement up to the report date.
items.applicability: Financial-reporting module; financial position at a point in time.
items.example: Balance Sheet 2026-12-31: Assets 150000 = Liabilities 100000 + Equity 50000 (incl. net profit 50000).
items.limitation: The identity only holds if P&L net profit is folded into equity; a Balance Sheet that omits current-year profit will not balance. It reports a snapshot, not movement.

items.title: Map every posting document to a single ledger primitive
items.description: Generate all posting documents (invoices, payments, stock entries, payroll) so that on submit they compile to a balanced General Ledger entry through one shared posting service. Do not let each document write GL rows with bespoke logic; centralise the debit/credit emission and the balance check.
items.applicability: Generator architecture decision applied across the whole accounting suite.
items.example: Sales Invoice, Purchase Invoice and Payment Entry all call PostToLedger(lines) which enforces sum(dr)==sum(cr) once.
items.limitation: A central posting service is a single point of failure if untested; it must be the most heavily unit-tested component and must reject any caller-supplied unbalanced line set.

items.title: Emit document status as an explicit state machine
items.description: Generate each transactional document with an explicit status field and allowed transitions: Draft -> Submitted -> Cancelled (and Draft -> Cancelled). Only Draft is editable; Submitted posts ledger/stock; Cancelled posts the exact reversal. Block any transition not in the allowed set.
items.applicability: Document-lifecycle scaffolding shared by all sales, purchase and stock documents.
items.example: Sales Invoice in Submitted state: Edit disabled, Cancel allowed (posts reversal), re-submit forbidden.
items.limitation: The state machine governs lifecycle, not business validity; a Submitted document can still be wrong. Cancellation must reverse every side effect (GL and stock), not just flip the flag.

items.title: Generate due-date and ageing fields on every receivable/payable
items.description: Every invoice (sales or purchase) must carry posting_date, due_date and outstanding_amount so that ageing reports (current, 30, 60, 90+ days) can be produced without recomputation. due_date derives from payment terms applied to posting_date.
items.applicability: AR/AP module data model; prerequisite for collections and payables ageing.
items.example: SINV posting_date 2026-05-01, terms Net 30 -> due_date 2026-05-31; on 2026-07-01 it is 31 days overdue.
items.limitation: Ageing buckets and 'as-of' logic are reporting policy; storing only a paid/unpaid flag without due_date makes correct ageing impossible to reconstruct later.

items.title: Tie sub-ledgers to GL control accounts
items.description: Generate AR, AP and Inventory as sub-ledgers whose totals must always equal their GL control account balance. Each party/item transaction updates both the sub-ledger detail and the control account through the same posting, so a reconciliation report can prove sub-ledger == control at any time.
items.applicability: Accounting integrity across the suite; the basis for AR/AP/stock reconciliation reports.
items.example: Sum of all customer outstanding balances (AR sub-ledger) == Accounts Receivable control account balance in the GL.
items.limitation: Divergence between sub-ledger and control usually signals a direct journal to the control account bypassing the sub-ledger; such journals should be blocked or flagged.

items.title: Generate a Credit Note as a reversing sales document
items.description: Emit a Credit Note (return/correction) as a negative or reversing Sales Invoice. On submit it reverses revenue and receivable: Debit Sales Revenue, Debit Output Tax, Credit Accounts Receivable; optionally restock returned goods. Link it to the original invoice for audit traceability.
items.applicability: Sales corrections and returns; the approved way to amend a posted invoice.
items.example: CN-2026-00001 against SINV-2026-00001 for 1 returned WidgetA: Dr Sales 100 / Dr Output Tax 15 / Cr AR 115; stock +1.
items.limitation: A credit note corrects accounting but stock restocking is conditional on a physical return; financial-only credit notes must not silently move inventory.

items.title: Generate a Debit Note as a reversing purchase document
items.description: Emit a Debit Note (supplier return/correction) as a reversing Purchase Invoice: Debit Accounts Payable, Credit Inventory/Expense, Credit Input Tax. Link it to the original purchase invoice and optionally reverse the stock receipt for returned goods.
items.applicability: Purchase corrections and supplier returns.
items.example: DBN-2026-00001 against PINV-2026-00001 for 2 returned RawX: Dr AP 115 / Cr Inventory 100 / Cr Input Tax 15; stock -2.
items.limitation: Tax reversal rules vary by jurisdiction; the generator emits the mechanical reversal but does not assert the supplier credit is legally valid for tax reclaim.

items.title: Resolve tax via a reusable tax template, not hard-coded rates
items.description: Generate taxes through a named Tax Template (rate, account, inclusive/exclusive) referenced by document lines, rather than embedding rates in document logic. The template computes the tax amount and the GL tax account so rate changes are a data change, not a code change.
items.applicability: Tax handling across sales and purchase documents.
items.example: Template 'Standard 15%' on a 1000 line yields 150 tax to Output Tax account; changing to 17% updates only the template.
items.limitation: Compound taxes, tax-on-tax, withholding and reverse-charge need richer template logic; a flat single-rate template is a starting skeleton, not a full tax engine.

items.title: Generate Customer and Supplier as parties with control mapping
items.description: Emit Customer and Supplier master entities that each map to a receivable/payable control account and a default currency, price list and tax template. Transactional documents reference the party, and the party's defaults seed new documents to reduce entry error.
items.applicability: Master-data scaffolding underpinning the whole transactional suite.
items.example: Customer ACME: AR control 'Debtors', currency USD, price list 'Standard', tax 'Standard 15%' default onto new invoices.
items.limitation: Master defaults are conveniences, not constraints; a document may legitimately override them, so validation must check the document values, not assume the master defaults were used.

items.title: Generate a Cost Center / dimension on postings for segment reporting
items.description: Allow each GL line to carry an optional accounting dimension (cost center, project, department) so P&L and other reports can be sliced by segment without separate ledgers. The dimension is captured at posting and flows from the source document.
items.applicability: Management-reporting capability layered onto the posting model.
items.example: JE rent 1000 tagged Cost Center 'Branch-North'; P&L by cost center attributes 1000 of rent to Branch-North.
items.limitation: Dimensions enable analysis but do not change the double-entry totals; mandatory-dimension enforcement is a separate policy and unbalanced dimension allocation can mislead segment P&L.
